reason: The statement applies because the text depicts Jonah as a righteous man who fears the Lord and follows His commands, even though he initially tries to flee. Yahweh protects Jonah by sending a great fish to save him from drowning in the sea, showing mercy and providing a second chance for Jonah to fulfill his mission. Additionally, Yahweh also shows mercy towards the repentant people of Nineveh, sparing them from destruction when they turn from their evil ways. This demonstrates Yahweh's protection of the righteous and His willingness to forgive the repentant, while also punishing the wicked who do not heed His warnings.
